channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Anthropic extends Claude Fable 5 access and Claude Code rate limits
item.description: The global extension for paid subscribers runs through July 19.
item.content:encoded:
The global extension for paid subscribers runs through July 19.

Original source: https://www.thehansindia.com/tech/how-to-buy-gmail-accounts-safely-in-2026-pva-aged-1067917

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/95ygdcdl

  • Views: 2,589,255
  • Likes: 49,382
  • Bookmarks: 1,847
  • Quotes: 254
  • Replies: 2,444
  • Posts: 66

Top authors

  • Eric Jang
  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • Jason Lee
  • Boaz Barak
  • Sebastian Raschka
thank you tibo

item.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella describes AI reverse information paradox
item.description: Box CEO Aaron Levie argues businesses must build infrastructure to protect proprietary IP.
item.content:encoded:
Box CEO Aaron Levie argues businesses must build infrastructure to protect proprietary IP.

Original source: http://Eve.dev

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/u4a9rpdr

  • Views: 4,358,903
  • Likes: 9,956
  • Bookmarks: 13,189
  • Quotes: 772
  • Replies: 702
  • Posts: 12

Top authors

  • Liam Fedus
  • Aravind Srinivas
  • Sriram Krishnan
  • Satya Nadella
  • Aaron Levie
"I find it ironic that the status quo is to then turn around and impose restrictive terms on distillation, and to reserve the right to learn from customer usage and interaction data. If learning flows in only one direction, economic value converges toward the owners of the learning infrastructure rather than the creators of the knowledge itself. Therefore, it's imperative that we distribute the learning infrastructure to every firm so that they can control their own learning loop." Well said.

item.title: ChatGPT Work temporarily removes five-hour limit to fix rate issues
item.description: Efficiency updates target GPT 5.6 Sol to reduce token consumption.
item.content:encoded:
Efficiency updates target GPT 5.6 Sol to reduce token consumption.

Original source: https://x.com/thsottiaux/status/2076365965915467978

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ah08zkwg

  • Views: 3,059,739
  • Likes: 33,948
  • Bookmarks: 2,974
  • Quotes: 1,964
  • Replies: 3,870
  • Posts: 15

Top authors

  • Jason Lee
  • kache
  • Tibo
  • Andrew Curran
  • Chris Albon
Not only did they remove the 5-hour limit, but my rates were completely reset again. OpenAI is doing incredibly good community work right now. Kudos. Absolutely outstanding community work.

item.title: Commentators argue legacy tech publications face declining readership after shifting from celebrating innovation to highly critical coverage
item.description: Critics suggest the negative coverage creates space for optimistic tech outlets.
item.content:encoded:
Critics suggest the negative coverage creates space for optimistic tech outlets.

Original source: https://www.wired.com/story/the-1x-neo-robot-has-freaky-fast-fingers/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ws7pqu05

  • Views: 105,366
  • Likes: 657
  • Bookmarks: 93
  • Quotes: 5
  • Replies: 63
  • Posts: 13

Top authors

  • Joshua Achiam
  • Lulu Cheng Meservey
  • andrew chen
  • Jason Crawford
  • Trae Stephens
You know I love you Josie, but this is a bad take. Haha. The problem isn’t negative press, it’s tabloid gossip masquerading as a credible tech publication, which @WIRED used to be. It’s not “criticism”, it’s a story about robot finger sexy time. This is not serious.

item.title: ClickUp AI head Jay Hack proposes open-air active noise cancellation
item.description: An interactive simulation models speaker arrays neutralizing construction site noise.
item.content:encoded:
An interactive simulation models speaker arrays neutralizing construction site noise.

Original source: https://www.globalconstructionreview.com/building-in-a-bubble-huge-inflatable-dome-covers-entire-construction-site/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/rvvaw8xe

  • Views: 650,914
  • Likes: 2,712
  • Bookmarks: 1,052
  • Quotes: 38
  • Replies: 238
  • Posts: 18

Top authors

  • signüll
  • Yishan
  • Yohei
  • Jay Hack
  • Andrew Côté
The Kirchoff-Helmholz integral theorem says perfect noise cancellation is in theory possible You can eliminate an arbitrary wave source - but it requires a continuous "shell" of cancellation speakers

item.title: Atreides CIO Gavin Baker argues falling model costs boost infrastructure demand
item.description: Cheaper tokens trigger Jevons Paradox, driving massive demand shift to infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
Cheaper tokens trigger Jevons Paradox, driving massive demand shift to infrastructure.

Original source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/the-ai-race-is-shifting-from-bigger-models-to-cheaper-smarter-systems.html

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/eu9z68uv

  • Views: 1,157,734
  • Likes: 3,579
  • Bookmarks: 1,830
  • Quotes: 98
  • Replies: 287
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Amjad Masad
  • Gavin Baker
  • Vincent Weisser
  • Rohan Paul
Jevons Paradox is about to hit AI harder than almost any industry we have seen before. People once thought faster internet would simply let us load the same websites more quickly. That was not even close to what happened. Faster connections created video streaming, cloud software, online gaming, video calls, social media, and entire businesses that could not exist on slow internet. Every increase in speed created new reasons to use more bandwidth. AI will work the same way. Today, we mostly use models for chat, coding, search, writing, and a few business workflows. But once intelligence becomes cheap enough, fast enough, and reliable enough, it will be built into every process that involves a decision. The biggest AI workloads probably do not exist yet. They are waiting for the cost of intelligence to fall. It will create millions of new tasks that are currently too slow, too expensive, or simply impossible.

item.title: Sam Altman asks developers for Solana 5.6 sol projects
item.description: Altman promised a physical OpenAI archive gift for the winner.
item.content:encoded:
Altman promised a physical OpenAI archive gift for the winner.

Original source: https://x.com/sama/status/2076398253332140410

  • Bookmarks: 2,080
  • Quotes: 157
  • Replies: 2,140
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Sam Altman
  • Chubby♨️
@sama @bughuntergeek @Angaisb_ or @JasonBotterill will win this easily.

item.title: Elon Musk claims Grok 4.5 achieves Opus-class browser-use performance
item.description: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch confirmed strong agent performance in early testing.
item.content:encoded:
Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch confirmed strong agent performance in early testing.

Original source: https://x.com/Alezander907/status/2076144541669962060

@elonmusk I was literally just testing it for this usecase and it’s performing great. Grok 4.5 is an excellent foundation for agents

item.title: Elon Musk says Grok 4.5 ties for top SWE-Atlas-QnA score
item.description: The model scored 84, matching Codex GPT-5.6 Sol.
item.content:encoded:
The model scored 84, matching Codex GPT-5.6 Sol.

Original source: https://x.com/elonmusk/status/2076434607562531049/photo/1

@elonmusk back in the game. lfg

item.title: European researchers release Soofi S 30B-A3B Mamba MoE trained on 27T tokens
item.description: The model achieves competitive German benchmark performance and fully open-sources its pretraining pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
The model achieves competitive German benchmark performance and fully open-sources its pretraining pipeline.

Original source: https://x.com/effi288/status/2075904321707798699
